items: Promote the development of new players from an early age at each club affiliated with the FEG.

blocks.text: Foster friendship and camaraderie among players from the different affiliated clubs.

items: Encourage the values of golf: honesty, sportsmanship, and healthy competition.

blocks.text: Categories are determined by birth year/class published by the FEG at the start of the season.

items: ALBATROS – Ladies and Gentlemen: players who turn 12 or 13 years old in the calendar year.
EAGLES – Ladies and Gentlemen: players who turn 10 or 11 years old in the calendar year.
BIRDIES – Ladies and Gentlemen: players who turn 7, 8, or 9 years old in the calendar year.
BEGINNERS: under 7 years of age and/or players who, regardless of age, have recently started playing golf (developmental category defined by the FEG).

blocks.text: Junior tournaments will be held on the dates established by the FEG in the annual calendar. They will be played on Sundays. The FEG may reschedule dates or designate alternative days for organizational, weather-related, or extraordinary reasons, with official notification.

items: Registrations will be accepted by the FEG through its official platform (or any other means it provides) until 6:00 p.m. two days prior to the tournament (the Thursday before the tournament).
The host club will prepare the starting sheet and must send it to the Coach/Authority

blocks.text: FEG by Friday at 12:00 noon at the latest for review and publication.

items: The host club will also post the list of registered players and tee times on its local notice board.
Registration must be completed by the junior academy professional (or designated person) and must expressly indicate whether the player is participating as a Beginner according to their judgment.
The registration fee is subject to the terms and increases defined by the FEG.
The host club will collect registration fees, prepare scorecards, and conduct tee assignments in a timely manner.
All registration revenue belongs entirely to the host club.
BEGINNERS: 5 holes, stroke play, holes designated by the Coach/Authority FEG.
BIRDIES: 9 holes, stroke play, holes designated by the Coach/Authority FEG.
EAGLES: 9 holes, stroke play, holes designated by the Coach/Authority FEG.
ALBATROS: 18 holes, stroke play, course/tees designated by the Coach/Authority

blocks.text: FEG.

items: The Rules of Golf in force, Local Rules, and these conditions will apply. The

blocks.text: FEG Committee/Coach may provide minimal adaptations in Beginners and Birdies for developmental and pace-of-play reasons.

items: Due to weather, safety, or operational reasons, the Committee/Coach may adjust schedules, reorganize tee times, and/or modify the number of holes to ensure completion of the event (with official notification).
The start of the tournament will depend on the number of players, weather, or any other extraordinary situation.
Reception: the host club must welcome players with breakfast.
Practice: the host club must provide a practice area so that players may warm up with their professionals, if required, and provide

blocks.text: DRIVING RANGE OR IMPROVISED PRACTICE AREA, previously announced to the FEG.

items: Tee times and starting holes will be confirmed by the club together with the FEG coach to avoid excessive waiting.
Lunch: the host club will provide (at its expense) lunch for the players, which may be served as they finish playing, or at a time determined by the club.

blocks.text: In addition, it must offer 2 additional menus for players' family members.

items: The host club must have the course marked with the corresponding distances (point 9) two days before the tournament; for example, with paint marks on the fairways. This will allow players to practice on the course with their tee shots, and will serve as a reference for the FEG Coach to verify that they are correct.
The host club will be responsible for receiving scorecards and for processing and publishing/announcing the results, as well as presenting the corresponding prizes.
The host club is advised that flag positions should not be placed in locations that may be difficult for the players.

blocks.text: ALBATROS (Ladies and Gentlemen): standard club tees; if there is more than one tee, use the shortest for each category (for example: "Red for Ladies / White or Blue for Gentlemen", depending on the club). EAGLES – Gentlemen (Blue Tees)

items: Par 3: up to 140 yds
Par 4: up to 310 yds
Par 5: up to 430 yds

blocks.text: EAGLES – Ladies (Red Tees)

items: Par 3: up to 100 yds
Par 4: up to 220 yds
Par 5: up to 330 yds

blocks.text: BIRDIES – Gentlemen (White Tees)

items: Par 3: up to 80 yds
Par 4: up to 180 yds
Par 5: up to 270 yds

blocks.text: BIRDIES – Ladies (Yellow Tees)

items: Par 3: up to 70 yds
Par 4: up to 150 yds
Par 5: up to 240 yds

blocks.text: BEGINNERS (Distance stakes or markers)

items: Par 3: 50 yds (yellow)
Par 4: 100 yds (red)
Par 5: 150 yds (white)

blocks.text: Competitors must present themselves with a neat appearance and appropriate golf attire. The following are considered not recommended: shorts, collarless shirts, joggers, jeans, and any clothing the Committee deems inappropriate.

items: Caddies: carrying a caddie is not permitted, except in cases of physical inability; in that case, assistance may be provided only for carrying equipment.
Manual push carts are permitted. Battery-powered, self-propelled, or golf carts are not permitted.

Advice (Rule 10): in no category may advice be given or requested. However, markers may advise or instruct the player on procedures provided for by the Rules of Golf. They will also ensure compliance with the prohibition of advice to players.

blocks.text: Exception: as this is a recreational/developmental category that does not count toward AAG ranking/qualification, in BEGINNERS the player may receive basic instructional advice from one (1) designated companion (marker, parent, or professional), solely for learning and safety purposes. In this category, players may also be assisted by companions in carrying their equipment.

items: Permitted advice is limited to: explanation of basic rules, safety, order of play, etiquette, procedure, and general choice of shot/target (e.g., "aim for the middle of the fairway", "use one club less").

Not permitted: detailed green reading ("hit it 30 cm to the left of the hole"), exact line indications, precise distance calculations ("it's 87 yds"), club selection, or prolonged technical coaching that delays pace of play.

The marker and the FEG Coach/Committee will monitor that the exception does not affect pace of play; in case of repeated violations or abuse, the Committee may revoke authorization for accompaniment/advice for the player in that round.
This exception applies only to the BEGINNERS category and does not modify the conditions for Birdies, Eagles, and Albatros, where advice is prohibited under the Rules of Golf.
Scorecard submitted: a scorecard will be considered submitted when the marker delivers the official card to the

blocks.text: Committee/Scorecard Reception. Markers / scorecards

items: BEGINNERS, BIRDIES, and EAGLES categories: will be accompanied by a marker provided by the host club to record the scores of all participants (the club must provide the full number of markers).

Markers may not be relatives of the players in the group, except that if markers are unavailable and the tee time is at risk, two parents/relatives may act as markers through double control and cross-marking (they may not mark for their own relative but may mark for their group partner(s)).
ALBATROS category players: will carry individual scorecards among themselves, each acting as marker for the other; there may be a supervisor in the group.
Markers may not give advice (exception: Beginners category), but may indicate procedures provided for by the Rules.

blocks.text: Maximum score (pace of play)

items: In the BEGINNERS and BIRDIES categories, the maximum number of strokes to be recorded per hole will be 10; however, the marker must allow the player to finish the hole, if they prefer, just like their playing partners.
Use of cell phones/audio devices is prohibited while playing on the course or near tee boxes or greens where other competitors are playing.

blocks.text: Players must observe proper conduct throughout the tournament, both on and off the golf course. The following acts will be considered serious breaches of conduct:

items: Throwing clubs or unsportsmanlike gestures.
Failure to comply with rulings/decisions of the Committee/Coach.
Disrespect toward officials, markers, or other players.
Leaving the course during competition and/or failing to submit the scorecard without cause duly justified before the tournament authorities.
Any act of indiscipline within the club facilities.

blocks.text: Penalties: any breach of the foregoing rules will be penalized by the tournament authorities, or by the FEG, as the sole competent authorities and with the involvement of the player concerned and their parents, with the following penalties:

items: Warning.
Disqualification from the competition.
Non-admission to future competitions organized by the FEG.

blocks.text: All tie-breakers to determine first and second place will be resolved by automatic countback to the first difference. BEGINNERS (5 holes): To determine 1st and 2nd place, ties will be resolved as follows:
1. Best score on the last hole played (hole 5).
2. If still tied, best score on hole 4, then hole 3, then hole 2, then hole 1 (first
difference).
3. If the tie still persists, it will be decided by draw conducted by the FEG Committee/Coach.

Note: for developmental and organizational reasons, on-course tie-breakers will not be played in the BEGINNERS category, unless the Committee/Coach exceptionally decides otherwise for operational reasons. BIRDIES and EAGLES (9 holes): To determine 1st and 2nd place, ties will be resolved as follows:
1. Best score on the last 6 holes (holes 4 through 9).
2. If still tied: best score on the last 3 holes (holes 7 through 9).
3. If still tied: hole-by-hole comparison from hole 9 to hole 1 until the first difference.
4. If there is still no difference, it will be decided on course at a hole 100 yards in length, with

the winner being the player who takes the fewest strokes. The Federación Entrerriana de Golf may, in case of force majeure or to expedite the tournament, use a simpler on-course tie-break method or a draw. ALBATROS (18 holes): To determine 1st and 2nd place, ties will be resolved as follows:
1. Best score on the last 9 holes (holes 10 through 18).
2. If still tied: best score on the last 6 holes (13 through 18).
3. If still tied: best score on the last 3 holes (16 through 18).
4. If still tied: compare from hole 18 to hole 1 until the first difference.
5. If there is still no difference, it will be decided on course by "sudden death", at the first hole of

difference. The Committee defines the sequence of holes. The Federación Entrerriana de Golf may, in case of force majeure or to expedite the tournament, use a simpler on-course tie-break method or a draw.

This ranking will be used to designate the players who will represent the FEG in the National Junior Tournament. The number of qualifiers depends on the quota granted by the AAG in each category and for each Federation.

items: Points awarded by position, from first to sixth place: 10, 8, 6, 4, 2, 1.

In case of a tie between two or more players, for the annual ranking the positions are considered tied. The points to be awarded for the tied positions are added together and divided by the number of tied players. The result will be rounded up to the nearest whole number in case of a decimal.
The scheduled calendar will consist of 6 in-person dates plus one virtual date that will count as a bonus.
The annual ranking will be compiled using a maximum of 4 (four) in-person dates, considering the best performances and discarding any dates exceeding this number.
The virtual date awards 50% of regular points and is not discarded: it is added to the total once discards have been applied.

The FEG reserves the right to schedule new dates during the year and to consider them countable or not for the purposes of compiling the Annual Ranking, which must be duly communicated through all available means to affiliated clubs.

blocks.text: Annual ranking tie-breaker:
1) greatest number of 1st-place finishes; 2) greatest number of 2nd-place finishes; 3) greatest number of 3rd
place finishes; 4) best result in the last countable date; 5) draw.

items: 1st and 2nd place in each category will receive prizes.

blocks.text: Additionally, all participants will receive a trophy, commemorative medal, or some "souvenir" as a memento of their participation. Raffles may also be held among participants for golf items, educational games, etc. These will be provided by the host club.

items: To encourage team play, the FEG will award an annual Cup, to be presented on the final date to the winning club. Each club will accumulate on each date the total points earned by its players.

blocks.text: The FEG will provide each host club, for its assigned tournament, the prizes required for all categories.
A host club that fails to comply with these regulations may not be considered by the FEG when preparing the following year's calendar.

items: No coach/professional/family member/spectator may approach or have contact with the player during the round except to assist when strictly necessary (water, food, medical condition). They must maintain an approximate distance of no less than 30 meters.
Failure to observe the foregoing will create a strong presumption of a breach of

blocks.text: Rule 10 (Advice), which carries a two-stroke penalty.

items: Markers will be responsible for informing the Federación Entrerriana de Golf Coach if they observe any breach of this rule.

The FEG reserves the right to take additional measures against family members, companions, spectators, or professionals who violate these provisions or who display unacceptable behavior toward players, markers, competition officials, or third parties in general.
